name: Jim Pepper
abstract: Jim Gilbert Pepper II (June 18, 1941 – February 10, 1992) was an American jazz saxophonist, composer and singer of Kaw and Muscogee heritage. He moved to New York City in 1964, where he came to prominence in the late 1960s as a member of The Free Spirits, an early jazz-rock fusion group that also featured Larry Coryell and Bob Moses.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Charlie Haden's Liberation Music Orchestra/Philly Joe Jones' Dameronia

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: That was a different thing... Jim Pepper, those guys... Pepper couldn’t make it, because I guess he was up for it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I was going to have him play on this piece, but he passed away in that week.
